Remedies: Rest in a dark, silent room. Apply cold compress on forehead/neck. Stay hydrated with water/electrolytes. Take prescribed triptan/NSAID if available. Practice slow breathing.
Precautions: Avoid bright lights and loud noises. Do not skip meals. Limit screen time. Identify triggers like caffeine, stress, lack of sleep. Seek help if worsens or >72 hours.

Remedies: Rest in a dark, silent room. Apply cold compress on forehead/neck. Stay hydrated with water/electrolytes. Take prescribed triptan/NSAID if available. Practice slow breathing.
Precautions: Avoid bright lights and loud noises. Do not skip meals. Limit screen time. Identify triggers like caffeine, stress, lack of sleep. Seek help if worsens or >72 hours.

Remedies: Lie down in a dark, quiet room. Cold compress on temples. Sip water slowly. Take OTC ibuprofen/paracetamol if safe. Gentle temple massage.
Precautions: Avoid caffeine, alcohol, artificial sweeteners. Do not strain eyes. Take 5-min screen breaks. Consult doctor if >4 migraines/month.

Remedies: Dark quiet room. Cold compress. Hydrate. Take prescribed migraine medication. Rest 1-2 hours.
Precautions: No screen time. Avoid perfume and smoke. Do not drive. Seek emergency if worst headache ever.

Remedies: Dark room rest. Cold compress. Hydrate with electrolytes. OTC pain reliever. Scalp massage. Use blue-light filter on screens.
Precautions: Avoid delayed meals and dehydration. No loud music or flashing lights. Do not drive during aura. Limit phone use.

Remedies: Apply warm compress on neck and shoulders. Gentle neck rolls (5 reps). Deep breathing for 5 minutes. Maintain upright posture. OTC pain reliever if needed.
Precautions: Take 5-min break every hour from screens. Avoid jaw clenching. Do not work in poor lighting. Stretch every 30 minutes. See doctor if >7 days.

Remedies: Warm compress. Neck stretches (ear-to-shoulder). Relaxation breathing (4-4-6). Hydrate. Correct sitting posture.
Precautions: Avoid prolonged sitting. Do not skip sleep. Reduce caffeine gradually. Avoid stressful conversations. Seek evaluation if daily.

Remedies: Warm compress to temples and neck. Shoulder rolls. Deep breathing. OTC pain reliever if needed. Stretch every hour.
Precautions: Avoid teeth clenching. Reduce screen brightness. Do not hunch over phone. Take breaks from reading.

Remedies: Apply warm pack to back of neck. Gentle neck stretches. Hydrate. Relax in quiet room for 15 minutes. Correct posture.
Precautions: Avoid loud environments. Do not skip meals. Reduce stress triggers. If daily, see doctor.

Remedies: Sit upright. Apply cold compress to eye area. Breathe oxygen if prescribed. Use triptan injection or nasal spray. Stay still in dark room.
Precautions: Do not lie down. Avoid alcohol during cluster. OTC painkillers are ineffective. Seek emergency if first episode.

Remedies: Stand or sit upright. Cold compress over eye or cheek. Oxygen if available. Take prescribed acute medication. Pace slowly if restless.
Precautions: No alcohol during entire cluster period. Do not take sleeping pills. Avoid high altitudes. Seek emergency if uncontrolled.

Remedies: Apply cold compress. Sit in a chair, lean forward. Breathe deeply but calmly. Use prescribed nasal spray.
Precautions: Do not consume alcohol or nitrates. Avoid strong perfumes. See doctor for preventive medication. Do not drive during attack.

Remedies: Use saline spray or rinse. Apply warm compress on forehead and cheeks. Steam inhalation 5-10 minutes. Hydrate. Sleep with head elevated.
Precautions: Avoid flying or diving. Do not use decongestants for more than 3 days. Avoid dry air. See doctor if fever >101°F or >7 days.

Remedies: Warm compress. Steam inhalation. Saline spray. Drink warm fluids like tea or broth. Rest with head elevated.
Precautions: Avoid bending forward rapidly. Do not smoke. Avoid cold drinks. Check for allergies.

Remedies: Nasal rinse with distilled water. Warm compress every 2 hours. Stay upright. Use humidifier at night.
Precautions: Do not use steroid sprays without prescription. Avoid alcohol. See ENT if recurrent.

Remedies: Apply ice or warm pack to base of skull and upper neck for 10 minutes. Chin tucks. Neck retraction exercises. Use cervical pillow.
Precautions: Avoid looking up or down for long periods. Do not carry heavy bags on one shoulder. Do not crack neck forcefully. See PT if chronic.

Remedies: Neck stretches: side bending and rotation (hold 15 seconds). Heat pack. Keep screen at eye level. Frequent stretch breaks.
Precautions: Avoid sudden jerky neck movements. Do not sleep on stomach. Limit phone use looking down. Seek help if numbness or tingling.

Remedies: Ice pack first for 10 minutes, then heat. Gentle isometric neck exercises. Maintain neutral spine while sitting.
Precautions: Avoid heavy lifting. Do not twist neck quickly. No high-impact exercise. See doctor if dizziness.

Remedies: Take steam inhalation with eucalyptus oil 3 times daily. Drink warm fluids like ginger tea, honey lemon, and soups. Use saline nasal spray. Rest with head elevated. Take OTC decongestant or cold medication.
Precautions: Avoid cold drinks and ice cream. Do not blow nose forcefully. Wash hands frequently. Stay home to avoid spreading. See doctor if >10 days or green discharge.

Remedies: Rest at home. Drink warm water. Take OTC cold medication. Use humidifier. Eat light nutritious food. Take vitamin C supplements.
Precautions: Avoid dairy if mucus increases. Do not share towels or utensils. Get flu vaccine annually. Cover mouth while coughing.

Remedies: Warm compress on face. Steam inhalation. Drink warm soups. Use nasal drops if prescribed. Gargle with warm salt water. Drink honey with warm water.
Precautions: Avoid AC and fans directly. Do not smoke. Cover mouth while coughing. See doctor if >10 days or green discharge.

Remedies: Take cough syrup as prescribed. Drink warm water with honey and ginger. Use lozenges for throat. Rest in upright position. Steam inhalation. Take OTC expectorant.
Precautions: Avoid cold food and drinks. Do not suppress cough forcefully. Cover mouth while coughing. See doctor if cough >2 weeks or blood in sputum.

Remedies: Warm saline gargle 3 times daily. Drink ginger tea with honey. Use cough drops. Rest. Keep room humidified. Take OTC cough suppressant.
Precautions: Avoid smoke and dust. Do not eat fried food. Sleep with head elevated. Seek medical if breathing difficulty. Avoid cold drinks.

Remedies: Honey with warm water and turmeric. Steam inhalation. Rest. Take OTC cough suppressant. Stay hydrated. Use throat lozenges.
Precautions: Avoid cold beverages. Do not overuse cough syrups. See doctor if fever with cough. Avoid spicy food.

Remedies: Gargle with warm salt water 3-4 times daily. Drink warm honey lemon water. Take lozenges. Rest voice. Take OTC pain reliever. Drink warm soups.
Precautions: Avoid spicy and acidic food. Do not whisper. Stay hydrated. See doctor if difficulty swallowing or fever >101°F.

Remedies: Drink warm liquids like soup and tea. Salt water gargle. Rest. Use throat spray if needed. Take paracetamol. Use humidifier.
Precautions: Avoid cold drinks. Do not smoke. Use humidifier. Seek care if tonsils swollen or white patches. Avoid dry food.

Remedies: Warm water with honey and lemon. Lozenges. Gargle. Rest voice. Stay hydrated. Take OTC pain reliever.
Precautions: Do not eat dry food. Avoid shouting. See doctor if >5 days. Avoid cold drinks.

Remedies: Take paracetamol or ibuprofen for fever. Apply cold compress on forehead. Rest in cool room. Drink plenty of fluids. Sponge bath with lukewarm water. Monitor temperature.
Precautions: Monitor temperature every 4 hours. Do not use cold water bath. Avoid crowded places. Seek doctor if fever >103°F or >3 days.

Remedies: Rest in bed. Take fever-reducing medication. Drink warm fluids. Use light blanket. Keep room ventilated. Eat light food.
Precautions: Do not overdress. Avoid alcohol. Do not skip meals. See doctor if fever with rash or stiff neck.

Remedies: Stay hydrated. Take OTC paracetamol. Rest. Use cool compress. Light diet (soup, khichdi). Monitor temperature.
Precautions: Avoid spicy food. Do not give aspirin to children. Monitor for dehydration. Seek care if fever >104°F.

Remedies: Take antiviral if prescribed. Rest in bed. Drink ORS and fluids. Take paracetamol or ibuprofen. Use warm compress. Sponge bath for fever.
Precautions: Avoid going out. Do not share utensils. Get flu shot yearly. See doctor if breathing difficulty or confusion. Rest completely.

Remedies: Rest. Hydrate with warm fluids. Take OTC medication. Use humidifier. Eat light meals. Take vitamin C supplements.
Precautions: Avoid alcohol and caffeine. Do not overexert. Monitor fever. Seek care if persistent vomiting. Get plenty of sleep.

Remedies: Warm fluids. Steam inhalation. Rest. Take medication for symptoms. Use warm compress. Take OTC pain reliever.
Precautions: Avoid crowded places. Cover cough. Wash hands. See doctor if symptoms worsen. Stay hydrated.

Remedies: Take antiviral medication. Rest completely in bed. Drink ORS and fluids. Take paracetamol for fever. Use warm compress. Gargle with salt water.
Precautions: Do not go out. Do not share utensils. Get flu shot annually. See doctor immediately if breathing difficulty or confusion.

Remedies: Rest. Hydrate with ORS. Take anti-nausea medication. Eat bland food (rice, banana, toast). Avoid dairy and spicy food. Sip fluids slowly.
Precautions: Do not eat heavy meals. Avoid sugary drinks. Wash hands frequently. See doctor if >3 days or blood in stool. Stay hydrated.

Remedies: Clear fluids (water, broth). Small frequent meals. Rest. Take OTC anti-diarrheal if needed. Oral rehydration solution. Eat BRAT diet.
Precautions: Avoid fried food. Do not self-medicate antibiotics. Stay hydrated. Seek care if severe dehydration. Avoid milk products.

Remedies: BRAT diet (banana, rice, apple sauce, toast). Hydrate. Rest. Avoid milk products. Drink ORS.
Precautions: Do not eat raw food. Wash hands. Avoid close contact. See doctor if fever >101°F. Avoid sugary drinks.

Remedies: Take antihistamine. Use nasal spray. Avoid allergens. Use cold compress. Stay indoors with windows closed. Use air purifier.
Precautions: Identify allergy triggers. Avoid outdoor activities in high pollen. Use air purifier. See doctor for allergy testing. Keep windows closed.

Remedies: Saline nasal rinse. Antihistamine. Use HEPA filter. Shower after outdoor exposure. Wash bedding weekly. Use hypoallergenic products.
Precautions: Avoid dusty areas. Do not rub eyes. Keep windows closed. See allergist if persistent. Avoid pets if allergic.

Remedies: Neti pot rinse. Steam inhalation. Take prescribed allergy medication. Use hypoallergenic pillow covers. Keep indoor humidity 40-50%.
Precautions: Avoid pets if allergic. Do not use scented products. Keep indoor humidity 40-50%. Consult doctor for immunotherapy. Avoid outdoor allergens.

Remedies: Eat iron-rich foods (spinach, dates, jaggery, beetroot). Take iron supplements as prescribed. Vitamin C enhances iron absorption. Rest. Stay hydrated.
Precautions: Do not drink tea or coffee immediately after meals. Get HB checked regularly. Eat small frequent meals. See doctor if HB <7. Avoid calcium with iron.

Remedies: Include leafy greens, pomegranate, and beetroot in daily diet. Take folic acid supplements. Rest. Avoid heavy exercise. Eat vitamin C rich fruits.
Precautions: Avoid calcium supplements with iron. Do not skip meals. Monitor HB levels monthly. Seek care if dizziness or fainting. Eat iron-rich foods.

Remedies: Eat iron-rich food (jaggery, dates). Take Vitamin C (lemon, orange). Rest. Avoid heavy exercise. Stay hydrated. Eat green leafy vegetables.
Precautions: Do not take iron on empty stomach. Avoid antacids with iron. Check HB regularly. Eat balanced diet.

Remedies: Take antacid. Drink cold milk. Eat small frequent meals. Avoid spicy and oily food. Sleep with head elevated. Stay away from caffeine and alcohol.
Precautions: Do not lie down immediately after eating. Avoid coffee, alcohol, and smoking. Manage stress. Eat at least 2 hours before sleeping.

Remedies: Drink coconut water. Take OTC antacid. Eat banana. Avoid citrus fruits on empty stomach. Stay upright after meals. Drink cold milk.
Precautions: Avoid fried foods. Do not wear tight clothes. Reduce stress. Avoid late night eating. Eat small meals.

Remedies: Take antacid syrup. Drink cold milk with honey. Eat light dinner. Avoid late night eating. Stay upright after meals. Avoid spicy food.
Precautions: Do not eat spicy food. Avoid carbonated drinks. Sleep on left side. Avoid smoking and alcohol. Eat small meals.

Remedies: Take digestive enzymes. Drink warm water with cumin seeds. Walk slowly for 10 minutes after meals. Eat light meals. Avoid gas-forming foods like beans and cabbage.
Precautions: Do not eat heavy meals. Avoid lying down immediately after food. Chew food properly. Avoid carbonated drinks. Exercise regularly.

Remedies: Drink ginger tea or fennel tea. Take OTC digestive aids. Use hot water bag on stomach. Eat small frequent meals. Walk after meals.
Precautions: Avoid cold drinks with meals. Do not overeat. Avoid processed food. Exercise regularly. Eat slowly.

Remedies: Chew fennel seeds after meals. Drink warm water. Light walk. Avoid carbonated drinks and beans. Eat small meals.
Precautions: Do not skip meals. Avoid stress. Eat at regular times. Avoid drinking water immediately after meals. Exercise daily.

Remedies: Drink 8-10 glasses of warm water daily. Eat high fiber foods (fruits, vegetables, whole grains). Take psyllium husk. Exercise 30 minutes daily. Include prunes and figs.
Precautions: Avoid processed foods. Do not ignore urge to defecate. Avoid excessive laxatives. Eat meals at regular times. Reduce dairy if causing issues.

Remedies: Drink warm lemon water in morning. Add ghee to meals. Eat soaked raisins and figs. Abdominal massage. Take stool softeners if needed.
Precautions: Do not delay bowel movement. Avoid straining. Limit cheese and processed foods. Drink water throughout the day. Exercise daily.

Remedies: Eat high-fiber breakfast (oatmeal). Drink prune juice daily. Take psyllium husk. Walk after meals. Eat raw vegetables and fruits.
Precautions: Avoid white bread and refined flour. Do not use laxatives regularly. Establish regular toilet routine. Drink room temperature water.

Remedies: Take warm sitz bath 3 times daily for 15 minutes. Apply ice pack. Take stool softeners. Eat high-fiber diet. Drink 8-10 glasses water. Apply OTC hemorrhoid cream.
Precautions: Avoid sitting for long periods. Do not strain. Avoid spicy and oily food. Do not delay bowel movement. Avoid heavy lifting.

Remedies: Use psyllium husk. Apply aloe vera gel. Take warm water baths. Eat cooked vegetables and fruits. Drink plenty fluids. Use stool softeners.
Precautions: Avoid constipation triggers. Do not sit on hard surfaces. Avoid reading on toilet. Reduce caffeine and alcohol. Exercise regularly.

Remedies: Eat fiber-rich diet (bran, oats). Drink psyllium husk. Use stool softeners. Take sitz bath daily. Stay hydrated. Eat leafy green vegetables.
Precautions: Avoid processed food. Do not delay bowel movement. Avoid spicy and fried food. Do not strain. Consult doctor if bleeding.

Remedies: Take ibuprofen or paracetamol. Apply heating pad on lower abdomen. Rest. Drink warm fluids (ginger tea). Light walking. Eat iron-rich foods.
Precautions: Avoid cold drinks. Do not skip meals. Reduce caffeine and salt. Avoid heavy exercise. Get adequate sleep. See doctor if severe pain.

Remedies: Use heat therapy on abdomen. Take OTC pain reliever. Gentle yoga stretches. Drink warm ginger tea. Eat iron-rich foods (dates, spinach).
Precautions: Avoid overexertion. Do not consume cold items. Eat small frequent meals. Stay hydrated. Avoid stress. See doctor if irregular.

Remedies: Apply warm compress on lower abdomen. Rest with legs elevated. Take pain reliever if needed. Drink warm water. Light walking.
Precautions: Avoid spicy and oily food. Reduce salt intake. Use heating pad. Avoid strenuous activities. See doctor if severe PMS.

Remedies: Drink ORS or water immediately. Rest in cool place. Apply cold compress. Sip fluids slowly. Avoid caffeine and alcohol.
Precautions: Do not ignore thirst. Avoid outdoor activities in heat. Drink water regularly. Seek care if dizziness.

Remedies: Sip water or ORS. Rest. Eat water-rich fruits (watermelon). Use electrolyte drinks. Avoid direct sun.
Precautions: Avoid direct sun. Do not heavy exercise. Drink water every hour. Monitor urine color.

Remedies: Drink plenty of water. Rest. Have lemon water. Eat juicy fruits. Stay in cool place.
Precautions: Do not skip water. Avoid salty food. Monitor urine color. Drink water regularly.

Remedies: Deep breathing exercises. Take a break. Walk in nature. Listen to calming music. Practice mindfulness.
Precautions: Avoid overthinking. Take regular breaks. Exercise daily. Seek help if persistent. Reduce screen time.

Remedies: Relaxation techniques. Talk to someone. Deep breathing. Light exercise. Take a walk.
Precautions: Do not isolate. Avoid caffeine and nicotine. Practice gratitude. See counselor if needed. Sleep well.

Remedies: Meditate 5-10 minutes. Journal thoughts. Take a walk. Practice progressive muscle relaxation.
Precautions: Avoid excessive screen time. Do not suppress emotions. Seek professional help if needed. Exercise daily.

Remedies: Apply warm compress on eye. Use antibiotic eye drops if prescribed. Avoid touching eyes. Wash hands frequently. Rest eyes.
Precautions: Do not share towels or eye drops. Avoid rubbing eyes. See doctor if vision changes or pain increases.

Remedies: Warm compress. Antibiotic eye drops. Avoid eye strain. Rest. Use artificial tears if needed.
Precautions: Do not wear contact lenses. Avoid touching eyes. See doctor if redness or discharge persists.
